author: CocaPls
description: Sync a hierarchical location tag (source of truth) into the filename prefix (tagkey). Cascade rename, sidebar tree view, vault bootstrap, and separator batch-change — all link-safe. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: CocaPls
manifest.description: Sync a hierarchical location tag (source of truth) into the filename prefix (tagkey). Cascade rename, sidebar tree view, vault bootstrap, and separator batch-change, all link-safe.
manifest.name: Trellis

name: Trellis

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
**0.3.2 — Nested tag view, root namespace, ID scheme presets**

Promotes the 0.3.x line to stable. Three new opt-in features:

- **Nested tag view** — a `notes | tags` view-mode toggle in the sidebar tree. Tag mode shows the full tag hierarchy folder-style across all namespaces, with an underline affordance on rows that carry a note, an untagged-notes section, and per-row label options.
- **Root namespace** (opt-in) — nest all managed tags under a shared root (e.g. `#root/trel/…`) via a vault-wide, cancellable, undoable tag migration. Filenames never change.
- **ID scheme presets** — per-slot segment schemes (`spark`, `zettel`, `date`, `seq`) that pre-fill the new-note modal (always editable) and let bootstrap parse single-run IDs.

Hardening from a full dual code review: new-note parent/segment validation (tag-illegal or YAML-breaking input gets a notice instead of a corrupted note; unicode segments fully supported), root-undo record retention (a cancelled undo can be retried), copy fixes.

Requires Obsidian **1.8.7+**. Tests 72/72. Defaults unchanged — all three features are off/opt-in; existing single-key vaults behave identically.

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
**0.3.1 — 0.3.0 features + the 0.2.2 lint cleanup** ⚠️ *prerelease*

Everything in 0.3.0 (nested tag view · root namespace · ID scheme presets — all experimental, opt-in), rebased onto the 0.2.2 compliance work:

- minAppVersion **1.8.7**; locale detection via the official `getLanguage()` only
- destructive-action buttons styled via a CSS class (no deprecated APIs)
- settings tab re-renders through an internal helper (no deprecated `display()` calls)

Stable line remains **0.2.2** (Latest). This prerelease is for testing the 0.3.x feature set.
releases.name: 0.3.1 (experimental)

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
**0.3.0 — Experimental: nested tag view, root namespace, ID scheme presets** ⚠️ *prerelease*

Three opt-in features on an experimental branch (`exp/0.3.0`):

- **Nested tag view** — a `notes | tags` view-mode toggle in the sidebar tree. Tag mode shows the full tag hierarchy folder-style across all namespaces, with an underline affordance on rows that carry a note, an untagged-notes section, and per-row label options.
- **Root namespace** (opt-in) — nest all managed tags under a shared root (e.g. `#root/trel/…`) via a vault-wide, cancellable, undoable tag migration. Filenames never change.
- **ID scheme presets** — per-slot segment schemes (`spark`, `zettel`, `date`, `seq`) that pre-fill the new-note modal (always editable) and let bootstrap parse single-run IDs.

Known: this tag predates the 0.2.2 lint cleanup — use **0.3.1** instead unless you need this exact snapshot.
releases.name: 0.3.0 (experimental)

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
**0.2.2 — Compatibility & lint cleanup**

- **Compatibility** — broadened API usage so the plugin no longer relies on version-gated APIs; destructive-action buttons (bulk apply / cancel) are now styled via a CSS class instead of deprecated button APIs.
- **Language** — locale detection uses Obsidian's official `getLanguage()` API only.
- **Internals** — the settings tab now re-renders through a private helper rather than the deprecated `display()` entry point.

No functional changes to sync, cascade, bootstrap, tree view, or the separator batch-change. Multi-key remains experimental and opt-in.

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
**0.2.1 — Settings UX & polish**

- **Settings** — merged slot editor button, staged Advanced-mode apply with a confirmation step, inline separator/schema validation, and a read-only **Managed notes** summary (how many notes carry a managed location tag, and the active namespaces).
- **Language** — locale detection now uses Obsidian's official `getLanguage()` (1.8.7+), with a fallback for older supported versions so Korean auto-detect still works.
- **API cleanup** — deprecated-API removal (`setCssProps`/`setCssStyles`, `setDestructive`); no static style assignments.
- **Docs** — README updated (EN/KO); minAppVersion documented as 1.4.10.

No functional regressions to single-key sync, cascade, bootstrap, or the separator batch-change. Multi-key remains experimental and opt-in.

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
**Multi-key & bulk UX** — experimental multi-key slots, a proper bulk-pass progress modal, and data-safety hardening. The single-key engine remains the default, battle-tested path.

### New
- **Multi-key slots (advanced mode, experimental)** — opt-in slot editor: multiple tag-key slots (each synced from its own namespace) + one free name slot, with per-gap separators. Off by default.
- **Bulk pass progress modal** — bootstrap & separator migration show a live count, progress bar, Pause/Resume/Cancel, elapsed time, and a Done summary listing skipped notes.
- **Quieter bulk passes** — per-file notices are suppressed during bulk runs; the modal shows aggregate status.
- **Explicit Apply in settings** — namespace/separator are staged and committed with an Apply button.
- **Settings grouped into sections** — General / Filename scheme / Sidebar tree view.
- **No-separator title protection** — tagging a no-separator note now prepends the tagkey and keeps your title instead of overwriting it.

### Hardening (multi-angle review before any real-vault use)
- Multi-key boundary matching is longest-separator-first (fixes permanent `AA01--BB02` corruption) and preserves a title's own trailing separators.
- Multi-key tagkey-only filenames stay intact (no `BT01-BT01`).
- Separator-change cancel rolls back cleanly; undo respects per-rename success and isolates per-file errors.
- Rename collision guard; namespace validation; collision notice fires once per clash.

Full details in [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md). Requires Obsidian 1.4.10+.
releases.name: 0.2.0 — Multi-key & bulk UX (experimental)

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
Frontmatter tag dedupe hotfix.

- Deduplicates exact repeated frontmatter tags while preserving order.
- Lets bootstrap normalize existing duplicate tag arrays when the target location tag is already present.
- Records bootstrap undo entries only for tags actually added by that bootstrap run.

Verification: npm test; npm run build; git diff --check.
releases.name: Trellis 0.1.4

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
Separator migration hotfix.

- Repairs mixed separator boundaries such as S88_-Title, S88-_Title, repeated variants, and suffix equivalents when moving between separators.
- Suppresses normal tag-to-filename sync during separator migration so follow-up events cannot re-process files with the wrong live separator.
- Records only successfully renamed files in separator undo data.
- Allows separator setting changes even when no files need renaming.

Validation: npm test (39/39), npm run build, git diff --check.

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
Marketplace review cleanup.

- Replaced workspace.revealLeaf() with setActiveLeaf(..., { focus: true }) so minAppVersion can stay at 1.4.10 while clearing the unsupported-API review error.
- Tightened saved-settings and frontmatter handling types around loadData() and processFrontMatter().
- Removed the builtin-modules dev dependency in favor of Node's built-in node:module builtinModules list.

Deferred warnings intentionally: getLanguage() requires Obsidian 1.8.7, and replacing display()/setWarning() would require 1.13.0 APIs.

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
Automated-review fixes for the community submission.

- **minAppVersion → 1.4.10** to match the APIs used (`processFrontMatter`, `AbstractInputSuggest`).
- Use `activeDocument` instead of `document` (pop-out window compatibility).

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/CocaPls/obsidian-trellis/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md).
releases.name: 0.1.1 — Marketplace review fixes

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
First public release.

- **Tag → filename sync** (link-safe): a hierarchical location tag drives the filename prefix (tagkey). Edit the tag; the filename follows.
- **Cascade rename**, **sidebar tree view**, **scoped bootstrap**, **duplicate location-tag cleanup**, **separator batch-change**.
- **Customizable sidebar view name** + **per-button header visibility**.
- **Korean / English UI** (auto-detected).

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/CocaPls/obsidian-trellis/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for the full list.
releases.name: 0.1.0 — First public release

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
- **Scoped bootstrap target picker** — onboard a folder/module subtree instead
  of only the whole vault. Checkbox tree with drag-to-select, filename search, a
  "notes without a tag only" toggle, a selection count, and already-tagged
  badges. Sidebar header buttons (bootstrap / rename tag / undo) and a "new
  note" command round out the entry points.
- **Duplicate location-tag cleanup** — one note = one location per namespace.
  Sync warns when a note carries duplicate location tags, and a new "check
  duplicate location tags" command opens a modal to pick which tag to keep per
  note; the rest are removed from frontmatter (undoable, with an "undo last
  duplicate-tag cleanup" command). The modal shows the total count, scrolls
  within the viewport, and batches at 50 notes for large runs.
- **Location tags belong in frontmatter** — settings and README now state that
  cascade and bootstrap read and rewrite frontmatter tags, one per note.
- **Robustness / cleanup** — every assembled vault path goes through
  `normalizePath()`; cascade rename now isolates per-file frontmatter errors
  (try/catch + skipped-files modal), matching bootstrap and separator change.
releases.name: 0.0.8 — Scoped bootstrap, duplicate-tag cleanup, robustness

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
## 0.0.7 — Separator batch-change + bulk-op robustness

- **Change the filename separator across the whole vault.** Editing the
  separator in settings now opens a confirm dialog and rewrites *only the
  trekey-boundary separator* on every location-tagged file — symbols inside the
  title are preserved — via the link-safe rename API. One-directional, like the
  tag engine. Includes a dry-run count + collapsible file list and a one-step
  undo command.
- **Separator validation relaxed** — any non-empty value with no letters,
  digits, or `/` (was "exactly one character"); multi-character separators are
  allowed.
- **Bulk operations are now robust and observable:**
  - **Per-file error isolation** — one broken file (e.g. duplicate YAML keys)
    no longer aborts a bootstrap or separator pass; failures are collected and
    listed in a "skipped files" modal.
  - **Live progress** — a progress notice (`N/total`) during bootstrap and
    separator change.
  - **Undo preserved on interruption** — the undo record is saved even if the
    pass is cut short.
- Internal: shared `assembleBasename` helper (slot order + separator) and
  `separatorMigratedName` (decompose with the old separator, re-emit with the
  new one).

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
## 0.0.6 — Multi-key data model (schema-based)

- **The filename key config is now a positional slot array** — `TrellisSchema
  { slots: KeySlot[]; separators: string[] }` — instead of three scalar fields.
  The single-key default is a 2-slot `[tag, name]` schema; runtime behaviour is
  unchanged.
- **`keyPosition` (prefix/suffix) is absorbed into slot order** — prefix =
  `[tag, name]`, suffix = `[name, tag]`. No separate field.
- **Existing settings migrate automatically** (`schemaFromLegacy`): saved
  `namespace` / `separator` / `keyPosition` data is converted to a schema on
  load, losslessly.
- Lays the general-form foundation for multi-key (multiple tag slots, multiple
  separators). The multi-key UI and multi-separator parsing are deferred to an
  advanced mode, so the core never needs rewriting again.
- Fix: settings no longer share the module-level default-schema object (an edit
  to the namespace/position could previously mutate the shared default).

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
## 0.0.5 — Internationalization (i18n)

- **Korean / English UI** via a small i18n layer (`i18n.ts`). Auto-detects
  Obsidian's UI language; a language setting can force `ko`/`en`.
- All commands, notices, modals, settings, and tree-view labels are translated.

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
## 0.0.4 — Bootstrap onboarding

- **Bootstrap an existing vault** (filename trekey prefixes, no tags yet):
  decompose a filename trekey into a hierarchical location tag
  (`S88B07` → `#trel/S88/B/07`). Placeholder slots (`0`/`00`) are kept as tag
  segments so the tag ↔ trekey round-trip stays exact.
- **Dry-run preview** command — lists every file's proposed tag (and files
  skipped because they're already tagged or have no recognizable trekey).
  Writes nothing.
- **Apply** writes the tag into each file's frontmatter (existing content
  preserved) and records what it wrote.
- **Undo last bootstrap** command reverts exactly those writes.

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
## 0.0.2 — Sidebar tree view

- Collapsible sidebar tree of the location-tag hierarchy (notes only;
  segment-only levels are transparent).
- Header actions (sort direction, collapse/expand all, reveal active file),
  sort options, and debounced refresh with a tree cache.

releases.author: CocaPls
releases.description:
## 0.0.1 — MVP

- Rename engine: a location tag drives the filename trekey prefix (link-safe via
  Obsidian's rename API), with an infinite-loop guard.
- Cascade rename of a parent tag (and everything under it) across the vault.
- Settings: namespace, separator, key position.
- Filename-edit restore (the tag is the source of truth) and title-key
  preservation on re-assembly.
